top_categories.workout_context: 5,900ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Granfondo Vosges' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
62 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Granfondo Vosges' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 62 miles.
95ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Granfondo Vosges, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Base fitness for Granfondo Vosges. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Granfondo Vosges. Train it until it feels boring.
5,900ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Granfondo Vosges, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 5,900ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: May in La Bresse, Vosges, France can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Granfondo Vosges. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Granfondo Vosges’s 62 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Granfondo Vosges with 5,900ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Granfondo Vosges requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 62 miles of mixed terrain in La Bresse, Vosges, France.
